description_long: This is a pair of classic metal pliers, featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles that converge at a central pivot joint. The jaws are robust and flat with a textured gripping surface, designed for holding, bending, or manipulating objects. The handles are uninsulated and appear to be made entirely of metal, lacking any ergonomic or rubberized covering. The pivot mechanism is a simple fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly. The surface of the pliers is matte and shows signs of wear, suggesting frequent use. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A sturdy, all-metal pair of pliers with flat, textured jaws and slightly curved handles, finished in a matte, worn surface.
description_view_0: A narrow, edge-on view showing the thin profile and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
description_view_1: A side view displaying the full length of the pliers, the curved handles, and the flat, textured jaws.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view from the opposite side, highlighting the thickness and straightness of the handles.
description_view_3: A mirrored side view showing the opposite profile, with the pivot and jaw design clearly visible.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, straight metal handles without insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, robust jaws with a textured gripping surface; no wire cutter or special notches present.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed single pivot joint, non-adjustable, standard for basic pliers.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal with visible wear, lacking any ribbed or knurled pattern.
